intro: Roulinia serratifolia, also known as the Brazilian clover tree or Serradifolia, is a plant species native to Brazil that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation, and also exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The bioactive constituents of Roulinia serratifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
